message:
fix(tests): use line-boundary split for Kafka section parsing in db/messaging baseline

The t68_kafka_* tests split the doc on "---" to extract the Kafka section,
but this matched the markdown table separator (|---------|) before reaching
the actual content rows and cancel-correctness paragraph. Using "\n---\n"
matches only horizontal rules on their own line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -759,7 +759,7 @@ fn t68_kafka_section_mentions_deterministic_fallback() {
     let kafka_section = doc
         .split("### 2.6 Kafka")
         .nth(1)
-        .and_then(|s| s.split("---").next())
+        .and_then(|s| s.split("\n---\n").next())
         .unwrap_or("");
 
     assert!(
